title: Import loss, stock build: How China absorbs the Hormuz shock
section_path: Import loss, stock build: How China absorbs the Hormuz shock
section_path_text: Import loss, stock build: How China absorbs the Hormuz shock
summary:
Main
China's crude market is doing something that, at first glance, doesn't quite add up. By Emma Li
As Hormuz-linked disruptions began to feed through in April-reflecting the typical four-week lag in Middle Eastern flows-seaborne crude imports fell sharply to around 8.4mbd, the lowest level since September 2022. This compares with an average of 11.1mbd in the first quarter and roughly 10.6mbd across 2025.

children.title: Main
children.summary:
China's crude market is doing something that, at first glance, doesn't quite add up. By Emma Li
As Hormuz-linked disruptions began to feed through in April-reflecting the typical four-week lag in Middle Eastern flows-seaborne crude imports fell sharply to around 8.4mbd, the lowest level since September 2022.

children.title: Runs, not imports, are doing the heavy lifting
section_path: Runs, not imports, are doing the heavy lifting
children.section_path_text: Runs, not imports, are doing the heavy lifting
children.summary: As highlighted in our earlier work [ read more here ], the adjustment began early. Refiners, particularly those heavily reliant on seaborne crude and with relatively thinner stock buffers, responded by cutting runs as early as early March, in some cases pulling forward seasonal maintenance.

children.title: The real constraint is not volume-it's quality
section_path: The real constraint is not volume-it's quality
children.section_path_text: The real constraint is not volume-it's quality
children.summary: At the same time, China is facing a structural shortfall of around 2.5mbd in non-Iranian Middle Eastern crude in April and May, relative to a 2025 baseline. In theory, long-haul barrels from the Atlantic Basin could help fill the gap.

children.title: China's hidden buffer: domestic and non-seaborne supply
section_path: China's hidden buffer: domestic and non-seaborne supply
children.section_path_text: China's hidden buffer: domestic and non-seaborne supply
children.summary: This is also where China's relative resilience becomes clear. Domestic crude production has quietly reached record levels, near 4.5mbd in the first quarter.

children.title: Sanctions waivers are temporarily reshuffling flows, not adding supply
section_path: Sanctions waivers are temporarily reshuffling flows, not adding supply
children.section_path_text: Sanctions waivers are temporarily reshuffling flows, not adding supply
children.summary: Sanctions waivers have added another layer of complexity to an already fragmented market-but their impact is often overstated. China's decision to issue additional crude import quotas to private refiners, shortly after imposing fuel export curbs, can be read as an implicit signal to sustain high-risk crude intake.

children.title: A market that bends, but does not break
section_path: A market that bends, but does not break
children.section_path_text: A market that bends, but does not break
children.summary: Taken together, the picture is one of a system under stress-but still functioning. China is not aggressively chasing alternative barrels to replace lost imports.
